_comment: Synthetic data. Fictional company 'Acme Billing'. No real PII. 30 messages spanning 2024-01-14 to 2024-05-09. The DM at slack_007 is the planted key memory for the Trace B reinforcement beat.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: Stripe webhook flaking — seeing duplicate `charge.succeeded` events processed twice. Sentry firing on /webhooks/stripe. Investigating.

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: On it with Alex. Looks like Stripe is retrying webhooks aggressively after a 5xx blip, and our handler isn't deduping on event.id. Eng Lead deploying fix tonight.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: Confirmed root cause — `webhooks/stripe.ts` was double-processing on retries. Patching now. ~12 customers double-charged so far, refunds queued.

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: Fix shipped — added retry handling to webhooks/stripe.ts. Stripe events flowing clean for 20 min straight. Postmortem tomorrow. Refunds processed for all 14 affected customers.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: Resolved. Marking incident closed. Will write up the postmortem in Notion tomorrow morning.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: Hey, been thinking about the Jan 14 Stripe thing. The fix we shipped only handles the simple retry case — we still don't have a cap on how many times a single event.id can be replayed.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: we should add a retry budget on idempotency keys, this'll bite us again

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: Agreed. I'll cut a WIP branch tonight and we can review tomorrow. Worst case is Stripe retries something 50 times after a network blip and we charge once per retry.

messages.author: ben@acme.io
messages.text: Billing service p95 latency is at 2.3s, alarms firing. Doesn't look Stripe-related — DB connection pool is saturated.

messages.author: sara@acme.io
messages.text: Confirmed it's the shared RDS box — noisy neighbor on the analytics workload. Failing over to read replica for now.

messages.author: ben@acme.io
messages.text: Latency back to baseline. Long-term fix is splitting the analytics DB. Filing a ticket. Closing this incident.

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: Design review at 4: Stripe webhook hardening. Proposing we standardize on the `X-Idempotency-Key` header for every webhook handler we own, not just Stripe. Doc in Notion.

messages.author: ben@acme.io
messages.text: +1. The Stripe SDK gives us `event.id` for free, we just need to pipe it into the same idempotency table the API uses for client requests.

messages.author: sara@acme.io
messages.text: Decision: X-Idempotency-Key header for Stripe webhooks, ADR-007 to be written. Owner: Ben. Target: end of next sprint.

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: Great — I'll review the ADR draft Friday. Don't forget to wire it through the retry path too, not just the first-attempt path.

messages.author: ben@acme.io
messages.text: Pushed ADR-007 to docs/ — feedback welcome by Wed. Mostly codifies what we discussed Thursday.

messages.author: sara@acme.io
messages.text: Welcome @jordan! New backend engineer joining the billing pod. First-week setup doc is in Notion.

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: Reminder: code freeze tomorrow 5pm for the v1.42 release. Get your PRs in.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: Anyone want to grab lunch at the new ramen place at 12:30? My treat if we ship v1.42 clean.

messages.author: ben@acme.io
messages.text: v1.42 deployed to prod. Smoke tests green. Can someone review #PR-1183 (refund flow refactor)? Small but I'd like a second pair of eyes.

messages.author: sara@acme.io
messages.text: Reviewed #PR-1183, left two comments. Mainly the error-mapping in `routes/refunds.ts` — let's not swallow the underlying Stripe error code.

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: Quarterly planning offsite is May 22-23 in Tahoe. Calendar invites going out today. Travel reimbursement form is in the wiki.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: Datadog dashboards finally migrated off the legacy v1 API. All billing-service alerts now route through the new pipeline.

messages.author: ben@acme.io
messages.text: Heads up — bumping Node from 20.11 to 20.13 across services next week. Mostly a security patch but worth a heads-up. Will run the canary first.

messages.author: sara@acme.io
messages.text: ICYMI: postmortem for the Apr 28 Redis hiccup is up in Notion. TL;DR: connection pool exhaustion on the rate-limiter. Mitigated, no customer impact.

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: Coffee chat at 3 with the new platform team — we're consolidating the deploy pipelines. Async notes will be in the platform channel.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: Anyone seeing slow CI on the billing-service repo this morning? My PR took 14 min to run. Probably Github Actions, not us, but logging it here.

messages.author: ben@acme.io
messages.text: X-Idempotency-Key header support is in main as of #PR-1207 — partial implementation. First-attempt path only for now; retry path TODO. Will tackle next sprint.

messages.author: alex@acme.io
messages.text: duplicate charges showing in Stripe — alarm fired at 03:47. Sentry event a3f8e9c1. This looks like the Jan 14 thing all over again.

messages.author: priya@acme.io
messages.text: ack, looking now
